content: Progress tracking and gamification pitfalls in learning apps: (1) Streak mechanics backfire — a single missed day or tech glitch causing streak loss is acutely demotivating; users feel punished for system errors. (2) Leaderboards shift focus from skill acquisition to ranking — competition over comprehension. (3) Extrinsic motivation collapse: when novelty wears off, points/badges lose power; only intrinsic motivation sustains long-term use. (4) Progress bars with large gaps feel impossible — must show noticeably forward movement each session. (5) Gameable metrics: any automated mastery signal (MMR, APM) will be optimized by users rather than learned against — e.g. boosting APM with spam clicks. (6) Sunk-cost visualization: showing incomplete progress on abandoned concepts can induce shame/avoidance rather than re-engagement. (7) Self-assessment bias: users consistently overrate their own mastery on conceptual topics; quizzes must have objective criteria.
